steps.title: Identify the grammatical requirements
steps.content:
The sentence reads: "The reed of a wind instrument is the mouthpiece ______." The blank completes a relative clause that defines what the mouthpiece is or does. Two rules apply: (1) a relative clause uses statement word order (subject before verb, not inverted), and (2) the full sentence is declarative, so it ends with a period.
  • Choice A — 'where is sound made.': Inverts the subject and verb ("is sound") as if it were a question, then adds a period — grammatically inconsistent. ✗
  • Choice B — 'where sound is made?': Has correct statement word order but ends with a question mark, which is wrong for a declarative sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — 'where sound is made.': Statement word order (subject "sound" before verb "is made") and ends with a period. Both rules satisfied. ✓
  • Choice D — 'where is sound made?': Inverts subject and verb and ends with a question mark — both errors present. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Choice C is the only option with correct statement word order and a period, making it grammatically complete for a declarative sentence. That matches choice C — C is correct.
